channel.title: Diogo Serino — Articles
channel.description: Visual breakdowns of product strategy, analytics, and business fundamentals by Diogo Serino, Product Manager & Builder.

item.title: How I Built TryCareerMatch
item.description: The full story of building TryCareerMatch — from a client-side prototype to a full-stack app: the problem, the scoring engine, the UX trade-offs, and the rebuild.

item.title: From Financial Analyst to Product Manager
item.description: What the transition from Financial Analyst to Product Manager actually looks like — what transfers, what doesn't, and how building TryCareerMatch proved it was possible.

item.title: What 7 Years of Design Gave Me as a PM
item.description: What a design background actually contributes to product management — the real advantages, the blind spots, and the habits that took years to unlearn.

item.title: BI Analytics Fundamentals
item.description: From raw tables to business insight — a breakdown of data modeling, DAX, SQL, and the KPIs that drive decisions in finance and HR.

item.title: Agile & Scrum Fundamentals
item.description: What Agile really means, how Scrum works in practice, the roles, events, and artifacts that make teams ship faster — and the metrics that prove it.

item.title: SaaS & Product Metrics
item.description: The metrics that define SaaS and product success — DAU/MAU, retention, churn, MRR, CAC, LTV, and how to choose the right ones for your product stage.

item.title: A/B Testing & Experimentation
item.description: How to design valid experiments, interpret statistical significance, avoid the most common testing mistakes, and build a culture of evidence-based decisions.

item.title: Prioritization in Practice
item.description: How to decide what gets built first — RICE, ICE, MoSCoW, Kano, and Opportunity Scoring explained with examples and a guide on when to use each.

item.title: Product Roadmapping
item.description: How to build a product roadmap — types, inputs, stakeholder management, how to say no gracefully, and the anti-patterns that destroy team trust.

item.title: North Star Metric
item.description: What a North Star Metric is, why it matters, how to choose one, and real examples from Spotify, Airbnb, Slack, and Facebook.

item.title: OKRs & KPIs
item.description: How Objectives & Key Results and KPIs work together — setting direction, measuring outcomes, avoiding vanity metrics, and building a culture of accountability.

item.title: Product Discovery & User Research
item.description: How to run user interviews, build personas, map empathy, apply Jobs-to-be-Done, and validate hypotheses before building — so you stop shipping what nobody uses.

item.title: Product Management Fundamentals
item.description: What PMs actually do, how the role differs from PO and BA, the product lifecycle, prioritization frameworks, and the metrics that define product success.

item.title: Business Model Canvas
item.description: How to map, analyse, and stress-test any business using the 9 building blocks of the Business Model Canvas — from value proposition to revenue streams.

item.title: Strategy Frameworks — SWOT, Porter's Five Forces & PESTEL
item.description: How to use SWOT, Porter's Five Forces, and PESTEL to analyse competitive position, industry structure, and macro environment — and when to use each.

item.title: Project Management Fundamentals — PMI vs Agile
item.description: The two schools of project management — PMI/PMBOK and Agile — how each works, when to apply them, and how hybrid approaches are used in practice.

item.title: Go-to-Market Strategy
item.description: How to launch a product or service — defining your ICP, positioning, channel strategy, and the metrics that tell you whether your GTM is working.

item.title: Growth Hacking Fundamentals
item.description: The AARRR framework, growth loops, key acquisition and retention tactics, and the metrics that separate real growth from vanity numbers.

item.title: Design Thinking Fundamentals
item.description: How to solve complex problems by starting with people — the five stages of Design Thinking, from deep empathy to tested prototypes.

item.title: Communication & Storytelling for Business
item.description: How to structure executive presentations, write for busy stakeholders, tell stories with data, and communicate across different audiences — the skills that separate good managers from great ones.

item.title: Digital Marketing Fundamentals
item.description: SEO, SEM, email marketing, and conversion funnels — how digital marketing channels work, how to measure them, and how they connect into a coherent acquisition system.

item.title: Startup Funding — From Idea to Investor
item.description: Pre-seed, seed, Series A — how startup funding rounds work, what investors look for, the difference between angels and VCs, and how to think about your cap table.

item.title: Economics Fundamentals
item.description: A visual guide to how the economy actually works — economic players, the circular flow, the role of banks and government, and macroeconomic fundamentals.

item.title: ESG & Sustainability — A Manager's Guide
item.description: Environmental, Social, and Governance — what ESG means for modern managers, how reporting frameworks work, and how sustainability is reshaping business strategy.

item.title: AML, KYC & Financial Crime Fundamentals
item.description: How banks detect and prevent financial crime — AML, KYC, sanctions screening, transaction monitoring, fraud, risk, and beneficial ownership.

item.title: Investment Fund Governance Fundamentals
item.description: How fund managers, ManCos, and depositaries keep billions in compliance — UCITS, AIFMD, NAV reconciliation, and the daily workflows of a fund Business Analyst.
